name: Syntax-Aware textarea

userStory.iWant: syntax highlighting while I edit code or config files
userStory.soThat: the editor feels professional and I can spot syntax errors instantly.

acceptanceCriteria: Light-weight highlighter for JSON, YAML, and Markdown
Customizable color themes via the Token Graph
Correct handling of multi-byte characters and line wraps
backwardCompatibility: The base textarea component remains identical; syntax highlighting is an optional opt-in prop.

inScope: Incremental update logic (only re-highlight visible lines)
Integration with the existing textarea cursor/wrap logic
outScope: Full IDE features (Auto-complete, LSP support — deferred to P3)

testPlan.goldenPath: Open a JSON file in the textarea. Verify that keys, strings, and numbers are colored according to the current theme.
edgeCases: Malformed syntax (should not crash the highlighter)
Nested languages (e.g. code blocks in Markdown)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Paste a 1MB JSON blob and measure the time to the first highlighted frame.
failureCases: Highlighter falling behind the user's typing speed (input lag)

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(L) where L is visible lines
